title: salvia pratensis var. lusitanica
intro: Salvia pratensis var. lusitanica, also known as Lusitanian meadow sage, is a variety of the perennial herb Salvia pratensis, native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Lusitanian meadow sage include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe digestive problems and reduce stress. Lusitanian meadow sage can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
